title: not working on ubuntu 20.04
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
Fresh ubuntu 20.04 install on a vps.

```
$ podman version
ERRO[0000] XDG_RUNTIME_DIR directory "/run/user/0" is not owned by the current user 
```

```
$ sudo podman ps -a
ERRO[0000] OCI Runtime runc is in use by a container, but is not available (not in configuration file or not installed) 
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                 COMMAND    CREATED      STATUS                  PORTS   NAMES
6281f0525b2a  docker.io/library/hello-world:latest  /hello     5 weeks ago  Created                         zen_curie
```

```
$ sudo podman rm zen_curie
ERRO[0000] OCI Runtime runc is in use by a container, but is not available (not in configuration file or not installed) 
Error: error removing container 6281f0525b2a304beeb41f7c7f5f56cf30d2e24eccab0e2bdec82d4f101f175c from runtime: runtime runc is missing: OCI not found
```

```
# podman ps -a
ERRO[0000] OCI Runtime runc is in use by a container, but is not available (not in configuration file or not installed) 
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                 COMMAND    CREATED      STATUS                  PORTS   NAMES
eda885d69ab8  docker.io/library/fedora:latest       /bin/bash  5 weeks ago  Created                         focused_bassi
```

```
# podman rm focused_bassi
ERRO[0000] OCI Runtime runc is in use by a container, but is not available (not in configuration file or not installed) 
Error: error removing container eda885d69ab8f50b7b761766539829619a8d6de0630c9773bb7b8fbf607c49e7 from runtime: runtime runc is missing: OCI not found
```

AS you can see, tests have been conducted with user and root. 
So Could you please enlighten me why this is happening on a fresh install ? related to the fact is a VPS? 
I'm not even sure the removal of container has worked.
or any action really?


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
Are you sure this is a fresh install? The `runc` error is usually seen after a system upgrade, or a similar situation where the OCI runtime on the system was swapped at some point in the past (container migration, maybe?).

Also - how are you logging into the non-root user? The `/run/user` errors usually happen when you try to `su`/`sudo` into a user, which does not establish a systemd login session. This can be resolved by logging in directly to the user via SSH or similar, which does create a login session, or running `loginctl enable-linger` on the user to force a login session to always be active.

well I don't see how it couldn't be a fresh isntall since the first thing I did is to install fail2ban and podman. 

But it could be because the image that have been installed by the provider is maybe the first 20.04. And If you are enclined to remember there have been quite a few upgrades to the container package in apt. 

I'm going to show you what happening by logging in directly through the user itself. And I didn't know about the problem with su (that too isn't very well showed in the troubleshooting part or documentation) but I understand your point. 

that is from an ssh connection directly into the user

```
redacted@redacted:~$ podman ps -a
ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "overlay" overwritten by graph driver "vfs" from database - delete libpod local files to resolve 
Error: cannot setup namespace using newuidmap: exit status 1
redacted@redacted:~$ sudo podman ps -a
[sudo] password for redacted: 
ERRO[0000] OCI Runtime runc is in use by a container, but is not available (not in configuration file or not installed) 
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                 COMMAND  CREATED      STATUS                  PORTS   NAMES
edb0d75dd9ca  docker.io/library/hello-world:latest  /hello   5 weeks ago  Created                         affectionate_varahamihira
ab6a099df197  docker.io/library/hello-world:latest  /hello   5 weeks ago  Created                         tender_chandrasekhar
6672487e14f9  docker.io/library/hello-world:latest  /hello   13 days ago  Exited (0) 13 days ago          pensive_pare
```

So what am I supposed to do now? 
Because it would be nice to have like a plan Z in the troubleshooting area where you know which package you need to purge if everything else failed and which folder you need to control needs to be empty.
Because I4ve made a purge podman but that didn't cut it.

And also @mheon, I've made several migrations from docker to podman and it never occured. So are I'm lucky and very unlucky in just this case, or something is missing. a package a configuration file. Something that the creators are supposing thtat the people have but could not be present in other cases.

I would recommend trying a `podman system reset` (assuming there are no containers or images on the system that you don't mind losing, since it's a fresh install) and then removing any configuration files in `~/.config/containers/`. It seems like something's wrong with the current configuration; if it's a clean install, it's probably easier to just wipe it and start from scratch, as opposed to trying to convince the current configurations to work.

totally yeah. 
So it seems it worked but since I've seen this :

```
$ sudo podman system reset

WARNING! This will remove:
        - all containers
        - all pods
        - all images
        - all build cache
Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] y
ERRO[0002] OCI Runtime runc is in use by a container, but is not available (not in configuration file or not installed) 
A storage.conf file exists at /etc/containers/storage.conf
You should remove this file if you did not modified the configuration.
```

I decied to remove everything
1/ make a copy of storage.conf in my home folder since it was complaining about that 
2/ so a purge of podman
3/ then a purge of container*
4/ removal of /etc/containers/
5/ removal of /root/config/containers

and there was no folder in my user for .config/containers 

Now it seems to work okey.
So or it is because I activated the user after having installed podman
or a deeper problem with one of the upgrade.
